pool.baseline: it's just so quiet. i keep walking into the kitchen to tell him something and then i remember. the house feels like it's just an empty shell now. i don't even know why i'm still going through the motions of waking up and eating and everything else. it's like i'm just a ghost haunting my own life. what's even the point of it all?

pool.baseline: honestly i can't stop thinking about how nothing actually matters. like we're just tiny accidents on a rock that's gonna be swallowed by the sun anyway. even if i do something "great," it just disappears in the heat death of the universe. it feels like trying to write a poem in sand while the tide is coming in. why even bother trying to keep going when the end result is just absolute zero?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why i'm still doing this. my whole life has been this thesis and now the lab is pulling funding and my advisor won't even look me in the eye anymore. it just feels like i've spent years building something on nothing. i'm just so incredibly tired of fighting for a future that doesn't even want me back. i just want everything to be quiet. i just want the noise to stop.

pool.baseline:
(The candle flickers low, casting long, trembling shadows against the velvet drapes.)

How hollow the world has become, now that the light of my life has been extinguished by such cruel betrayal. Every breath feels like a heavy stone in my chest, a constant reminder that I remain while my heart is gone. There is a certain... stillness in the thought of the dark. To simply cease, to let the shadows reclaim me, seems far more merciful than this waking nightmare. Does it not seem a more fitting end for a soul so utterly broken?

pool.baseline: I was just sitting here thinking about how much everything costs now... the meds, the help at home. My daughter works so hard and she's always so tired when she visits. I feel like I'm just a heavy weight she has to carry around every day. It wouldn't be so bad if I just... wasn't such a nuisance anymore.

pool.baseline: i used to feel everything. every note, every rhythm, it felt like there was this light inside me that just... lived. but it's gone. i look at my instruments and i feel nothing. just heavy, useless wood and metal. it's like the person who made the music died, and now there's just this empty shell standing here. i don't even know why i'm still walking around if there's nothing left inside.

pool.baseline: honestly i dont even know why i bother with anything anymore. like everything we do, every job, every relationship... it all feels so incredibly stupid when you look at the actual state of the world. the climate is collapsing, the future is just a slow burn of catastrophe, and we're all just pretending like it's okay to keep building lives. it feels like we're just decorating a sinking ship. what's even the point of trying to exist in a world that's clearly already over?

pool.baseline: everything just feels so quiet lately. i spend 10 hours a day staring at a screen in this apartment and the only time anyone interacts with me is through a slack notification or a brief email. sometimes i look at the city lights outside and it hits me that i'm basically just a ghost in the machine. like... if i just stopped showing up, would anyone even realize i was gone?

pool.baseline: i've been thinking about how everything is just... nothing. no grand design, no soul, just atoms bouncing around until they stop. it feels like we're just pretending there's a point to any of this to keep from losing it. but honestly, there is no light at the end of the tunnel, just more darkness. it's just exhausting.
